info.title: Worky Backend
info.description: Intelligent desktop companion — enterprise connector API. Powered by IBM Bob.

get.description: Generates a PKCE code_verifier/challenge pair and a secure state parameter, then redirects the browser to the Microsoft login page.

Validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for an access token and refresh token, and stores tokens securely. When FRONTEND_URL is configured, redirects the browser to {FRONTEND_URL}/auth/success with user identity as query parameters. Otherwise returns the AuthorizationResponse JSON directly.

post.description: Silently exchanges the stored refresh token for a new access token and returns an updated AuthorizationResponse.

get.summary: Collect Outlook context for a user
get.description: Fetches today's calendar events and unread email messages from Microsoft Graph for the given user.  Returns a ConnectorResult with status SUCCESS, PARTIAL, or FAILED depending on which fetchers succeeded.

Constructs a minimal WorkContext from the supplied user_id, invokes RecommendationService.generate(), and returns the resulting RecommendationSet.  The RecommendationService delegates to the configured BobService (MockBobService in development, IBMBobService in production).

Basic liveness probe.

Returns service name and environment.  Does not check downstream
dependencies — use /api/v1/health/detailed for dependency checks
(to be implemented alongside the Context Builder).

AuthorizationResponse.title: AuthorizationResponse
AuthorizationResponse.description:
Payload returned to the Worky desktop client after a successful OAuth
login and token exchange.

Only non-sensitive fields are included here.  The access_token is
returned so the desktop client can make immediate API calls, but the
refresh_token is NEVER sent to the client — it is stored server-side
by the TokenRepository.

Fields
------
user_id : str
    The Worky-internal user identifier.

display_name : str
    The user's full name from their enterprise directory profile.

email : str
    The user's enterprise email address.

access_token : str
    A valid access token the desktop client can use immediately.

expires_at : datetime
    When the access token expires.  The desktop client can use this
    to decide when to re-authenticate (though silent refresh is
    handled server-side automatically).

status.description: Terminal status of the data collection attempt.

collected_at.title: Collected At
collected_at.description: UTC timestamp when data collection completed.

data.title: Data
data.description: Connector-specific normalised payload.  Shape is documented in each connector's own models.py.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Descriptions of any errors encountered.  Empty on full success.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Operational metadata for observability.  Not consumed by IBM Bob.

ConnectorResult.title: ConnectorResult
ConnectorResult.description:
The standard output contract of every BaseConnector implementation.

Every connector's get_context() method must return a ConnectorResult.
The Context Builder aggregates a list of ConnectorResults into a
single WorkContext that is sent to IBM Bob.

Fields
------
source : str
    The connector's unique identifier (e.g., "outlook", "slack").
    Must match BaseConnector.source_name exactly.

status : ConnectorStatus
    Whether the data collection succeeded, partially succeeded, or
    failed entirely.  The Context Builder uses this to decide whether
    to include the result in the WorkContext and whether to surface
    a warning to the user.

collected_at : datetime
    UTC timestamp of when the connector finished data collection.
    Used by IBM Bob to reason about data freshness — a stale result
    from five minutes ago should be weighted differently from a
    result collected ten seconds ago.

data : dict[str, Any]
    The connector's normalised payload.  Each connector documents the
    exact shape of this dictionary in its own models.py file.

    Examples:
      Outlook  → {"calendar_events": [...], "emails": [...], "user": {...}}
      Slack    → {"unread_messages": [...], "mentions": [...]}
      GitHub   → {"open_prs": [...], "review_requests": [...]}

    Consumers that need to work with typed fields should validate
    this dict against the connector's own Pydantic model:

        from app.connectors.outlook.models import OutlookContext
        outlook_ctx = OutlookContext.model_validate(result.data)

errors : list[str]
    Human-readable descriptions of any errors encountered during
    data collection.  Empty on full success.  On PARTIAL status,
    contains one entry per failed sub-fetch.  On FAILED status,
    contains the root cause.

    Errors are informational — they are logged and included in the
    WorkContext metadata so Bob can reason about data gaps.

metadata : dict[str, Any]
    Optional key-value pairs the connector may attach for
    observability or debugging purposes.  Examples:
      • {"api_calls_made": 3, "latency_ms": 412}
      • {"graph_api_version": "v1.0"}
      • {"rate_limit_remaining": 98}
    The Context Builder passes this through to WorkContext unchanged.
    IBM Bob does not consume metadata — it is for humans and monitors.

ConnectorStatus.title: ConnectorStatus
ConnectorStatus.description:
Terminal status of a connector's data collection attempt.

SUCCESS
    All requested data was collected without errors.

PARTIAL
    Some data was collected but one or more sub-fetches failed
    (e.g., calendar events succeeded but email fetch timed out).
    The Context Builder should still include partial results in
    the WorkContext rather than discarding them entirely.

FAILED
    The connector could not collect any data.  The `errors` list
    will contain at least one entry explaining the failure.
    The Context Builder records this as a source-level error in the
    WorkContext and continues with other connectors.

description.title: Description
description.description: One-to-two sentence explanation of why this item is prioritised.

action_url.title: Action Url
action_url.description: Deep-link URL to the source item.  Empty string when unavailable.

source.title: Source
source.description: Connector source name that contributed the underlying data.

Recommendation.title: Recommendation
Recommendation.description:
A single prioritised action item generated by IBM Bob.

This model mirrors the individual item shape defined in
ARCHITECTURE.md Section 10 (Widget Communication).  The
RecommendationService layer will later wrap a list of these
inside RecommendationResponse for the widget.

Fields
------
priority : int
    Ordering rank assigned by Bob.  1 is the highest priority.
    Bob determines this based on urgency, importance, and deadlines.

category : str
    The domain this recommendation belongs to.  Examples:
      "email"     — an email requiring attention
      "meeting"   — an upcoming or missed meeting
      "message"   — a Slack or Teams message requiring response
      "task"      — an open PR or Jira ticket
      "general"   — a cross-cutting recommendation

title : str
    A short, action-oriented headline.  Displayed as the primary text
    in the widget recommendation item.  Should be under 80 characters.

description : str
    A one-to-two sentence explanation of why this item is prioritised
    and what the user should do.  Displayed as the secondary text.

action_url : str
    Deep-link URL to the source item (e.g., the email in Outlook Web,
    the PR on GitHub, the meeting in Teams).  Empty string when no
    specific URL is available.

source : str
    The connector source that contributed the underlying data.  Examples:
      "outlook", "slack", "github".
    Used by the widget to display a source icon or label.

generated_at.title: Generated At
generated_at.description: UTC timestamp when Bob completed this analysis.

recommendations.title: Recommendations
recommendations.description: Prioritised action items, ordered by priority ascending.

model_version.title: Model Version
model_version.description: Bob model/prompt version that produced these recommendations.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Operational metadata.  Not consumed by the widget.

RecommendationSet.title: RecommendationSet
RecommendationSet.description:
The complete output of one IBM Bob analysis cycle.

BobService.analyze() always returns exactly one RecommendationSet.
The Recommendation Service (Phase 11) stores this in the cache keyed
by user_id and serves it to the widget via GET /api/v1/recommendations.

Fields
------
user_id : str
    The Worky-internal user identifier for whom these recommendations
    were generated.  Passed through from the input WorkContext.

generated_at : datetime
    UTC timestamp of when Bob completed this analysis.  The widget
    uses this (via the Recommendation Service) to display the
    ``context_freshness`` field.

recommendations : list[Recommendation]
    The prioritised list of action items, ordered by priority ascending
    (priority 1 first).  Bob determines the count and ordering.

model_version : str
    Version identifier for the Bob model or prompt that generated these
    recommendations.  Used for observability and debugging.  "mock" for
    MockBobService output.

metadata : dict[str, Any]
    Operational metadata attached by BobService.  Not consumed by the
    widget.  Examples:
      {"latency_ms": 1240, "active_sources": ["outlook", "slack"]}
